input.description:
Use this tool when:
The user asks to attach files to an existing draft.
The user wants to include attachments before sending a draft.
A draft has already been created and attachments must be added separately.

Do not use this tool when:
Creating a new draft (use create_draft).
Editing body, subject, or recipients (use update_draft).
Sending the email (use send_draft).
No draft exists yet.

Ask the user to clarify which files to attach if attachment references are not explicit.

properties.label: Attachments
properties.name: attachments
properties.hint: At least one attachment must be present. Must include 'filename', 'mime_type' and (either 'file_id' or 'message_id' and 'attachment_id') for each attachment; do not invent attachment content.

properties.label: Source
properties.name: source
properties.hint: Must be one of: "gmail" or "gdrive". Use "gdrive" if the referenced attachment is a file in google drive. Use "gmail" if referred to an existing attachment in google mail.

properties.label: Filename
properties.name: filename
properties.hint: Required for each attachment: use a user-friendly file name including extension (e.g., report.csv).

properties.label: Mime type
properties.name: mimeType
properties.hint: Required for each attachment: provide the correct MIME type. For Google native files, use the exported format equivalent MIME type (not the Google-native MIME type).

properties.label: File ID
properties.name: fileId
properties.hint: Required if "source" is "gdrive". Must be the ID of the file in google drive.

properties.label: Attachment ID
properties.name: attachmentId
properties.hint: Required if "source" is "gmail". Must be the ID of the attachment within the provided "message_id".

properties.label: Message ID
properties.name: messageId
properties.hint: Required if "source" is "gmail". Must be the ID of the existing message, that holds the attachment.

input.code:
require "base64"
require "securerandom"
require "time"

base64url_encode = lambda do |str|
  Base64.urlsafe_encode64(str, padding: false)
end

base64url_decode = lambda do |str|
  s = str.to_s
  s += "=" * ((4 - s.length % 4) % 4)
  Base64.urlsafe_decode64(s)
end

crlf = lambda do |s|
  s.to_s.gsub("\r\n", "\n").gsub("\r", "\n").gsub("\n", "\r\n")
end

normalize_list = lambda do |v|
  return nil if v.nil?
  arr = v.is_a?(Array) ? v : [v]
  out = arr.map(&:to_s).map(&:strip).reject(&:empty?)
  out.empty? ? nil : out
end

html_to_text_fallback = lambda do |html|
  html.to_s
      .gsub(/<br\s*\/?>/i, "\n")
      .gsub(/<\/p>/i, "\n\n")
      .gsub(/<[^>]+>/, "")
      .strip
end

safe_param = lambda do |v|
  v.to_s.gsub(/[\r\n]/, "").gsub('"', '\"').strip
end

chunk76 = lambda do |b64|
  b64.to_s.scan(/.{1,76}/).join("\r\n")
end

header_value = lambda do |headers, name|
  return nil unless headers.is_a?(Array)
  h = headers.find { |x| x && x["name"].to_s.downcase == name.downcase }
  h ? h["value"].to_s : nil
end

decode_payload_text = lambda do |payload|
  data = payload.dig("body", "data").to_s
  return "" if data.empty?
  base64url_decode.call(data).force_encoding("UTF-8")
end

extract_existing_body = lambda do |payload|
  return "" unless payload.is_a?(Hash)

  mime = payload["mimeType"].to_s.downcase
  parts = payload["parts"]

  if mime.start_with?("multipart/") && parts.is_a?(Array)
    candidate = parts.find { |p| p["mimeType"].to_s.downcase == "text/plain" } ||
                parts.find { |p| p["mimeType"].to_s.downcase == "text/html" }
    return decode_payload_text.call(candidate) if candidate

    alt = parts.find { |p| p["mimeType"].to_s.downcase.start_with?("multipart/") }
    if alt && alt["parts"].is_a?(Array)
      sub = alt["parts"].find { |p| p["mimeType"].to_s.downcase == "text/plain" } ||
            alt["parts"].find { |p| p["mimeType"].to_s.downcase == "text/html" }
      return sub ? decode_payload_text.call(sub) : ""
    end

    ""
  else
    decode_payload_text.call(payload)
  end
end

build_body_part = lambda do |body, body_format|
  fmt = (body_format || "plain_text").to_s
  case fmt
  when "plain_text"
    [
      "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
      "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
      "",
      crlf.call(body.to_s),
      ""
    ].join("\r\n")
  when "html"
    boundary = "alt-#{SecureRandom.hex(8)}"
    plain = html_to_text_fallback.call(body.to_s)
    [
      "Content-Type: multipart/alternative; boundary=\"#{boundary}\"",
      "",
      "--#{boundary}",
      "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
      "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
      "",
      crlf.call(plain),
      "",
      "--#{boundary}",
      "Content-Type: text/html; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
      "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
      "",
      crlf.call(body.to_s),
      "",
      "--#{boundary}--",
      ""
    ].join("\r\n")
  else
    raise "Invalid body_format: #{fmt}. Use plain_text or html."
  end
end

attachment_to_mime_base64 = lambda do |input_value|
  original = input_value.is_a?(String) ? input_value : input_value.to_s

  if original.start_with?("data:")
    comma = original.index(",")
    raise "Invalid data URL (missing comma)" if comma.nil?
    original = original[(comma + 1)..-1].to_s
  end

  candidate = original.gsub(/\s+/, "")

  looks_like_b64 =
    !candidate.empty? &&
    candidate.length >= 8 &&
    candidate.match?(/\A[A-Za-z0-9+\/=_-]+\z/)

  if looks_like_b64
    begin
      b64 = candidate.tr("-_", "+/")
      b64 += "=" * ((4 - (b64.length % 4)) % 4)
      raw = Base64.strict_decode64(b64)
      return Base64.strict_encode64(raw)
    rescue ArgumentError
      # fall through
    end
  end

  Base64.strict_encode64(original.b)
end

update = (input["update_values"] || {})
existing = (input["existing_draft_data"] || {})
existing_msg = (existing["message"] || {})
payload = (existing_msg["payload"] || {})

draft_id = existing["id"].to_s.strip
raise "existing_draft_data.id (draft id) is required" if draft_id.empty?

headers = payload["headers"] || []

final_to = header_value.call(headers, "To")
final_cc = header_value.call(headers, "Cc")
final_bcc = header_value.call(headers, "Bcc")
final_subject = header_value.call(headers, "Subject")
final_subject = "(no subject)" if final_subject.to_s.strip.empty?
e_in_reply_to = header_value.call(headers, "In-Reply-To")
e_references = header_value.call(headers, "References")
final_body = extract_existing_body.call(payload)

existing_has_html =
  (payload["mimeType"].to_s.downcase == "text/html") ||
  ((payload["parts"] || []).any? { |p| p["mimeType"].to_s.downcase == "text/html" }) ||
  ((payload["parts"] || []).any? do |p|
    p["mimeType"].to_s.downcase.start_with?("multipart/") &&
      (p["parts"] || []).any? { |sp| sp["mimeType"].to_s.downcase == "text/html" }
  end)

final_body_format = existing_has_html ? "html" : "plain_text"

attachments = (update.key?("attachments") ? (update["attachments"] || []) : []).map { |a| a || {} }

headers_out = []
headers_out << "To: #{final_to}" if final_to && !final_to.strip.empty?
headers_out << "Cc: #{final_cc}" if final_cc && !final_cc.strip.empty?
headers_out << "Bcc: #{final_bcc}" if final_bcc && !final_bcc.strip.empty?
headers_out << "Subject: #{final_subject}"
headers_out << "In-Reply-To: #{e_in_reply_to}" if e_in_reply_to && !e_in_reply_to.strip.empty?
headers_out << "References: #{e_references}" if e_references && !e_references.strip.empty?
headers_out << "Date: #{Time.now.rfc2822}"
headers_out << "MIME-Version: 1.0"

body_part = build_body_part.call(final_body, final_body_format)

if attachments.any?
  mix = "mix-#{SecureRandom.hex(8)}"
  headers_out << "Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary=\"#{mix}\""

  parts = []
  parts << "--#{mix}"
  parts << body_part

  attachments.each_with_index do |a, idx|
    filename   = a["filename"].to_s.strip
    mime_type  = a["mime_type"].to_s.strip
    data_input = a["data_base64"]

    raise "attachments[#{idx}].filename is required" if filename.empty?
    raise "attachments[#{idx}].mime_type is required" if mime_type.empty?
    raise "attachments[#{idx}].data_base64 is required" if data_input.nil? || data_input.to_s.empty?

    fn = safe_param.call(filename)
    data_base64 = attachment_to_mime_base64.call(data_input)

    parts << "--#{mix}"
    parts << "Content-Type: #{mime_type}; name=\"#{fn}\""
    parts << "Content-Disposition: attachment; filename=\"#{fn}\""
    parts << "Content-Transfer-Encoding: base64"
    parts << ""
    parts << chunk76.call(data_base64)
    parts << ""
  end

  parts << "--#{mix}--"
  parts << ""

  rfc822 = headers_out.join("\r\n") + "\r\n\r\n" + parts.join("\r\n")
else
  if final_body_format == "html"
    boundary = "alt-#{SecureRandom.hex(8)}"
    plain = html_to_text_fallback.call(final_body)

    headers_out << "Content-Type: multipart/alternative; boundary=\"#{boundary}\""

    body_content = [
      "--#{boundary}",
      "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
      "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
      "",
      crlf.call(plain),
      "",
      "--#{boundary}",
      "Content-Type: text/html; charset=\"UTF-8\"",
      "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit",
      "",
      crlf.call(final_body),
      "",
      "--#{boundary}--",
      ""
    ].join("\r\n")

    rfc822 = headers_out.join("\r\n") + "\r\n\r\n" + body_content
  else
    headers_out << "Content-Type: text/plain; charset=\"UTF-8\""
    headers_out << "Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit"
    rfc822 = headers_out.join("\r\n") + "\r\n\r\n" + crlf.call(final_body)
  end
end

raw = base64url_encode.call(rfc822)
thread_id = existing_msg["threadId"].to_s.strip

draft_update_request = {
  "id" => draft_id,
  "message" => { "raw" => raw }
}
draft_update_request["message"]["threadId"] = thread_id unless thread_id.empty?

{
  "draft_id" => draft_id,
  "draft_update_request" => draft_update_request,
  "mime_preview" => rfc822
}
input.name: Create email mime
